creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents a five-pillar framework for deploying enterprise AI agents: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. He illustrates with a retail bank case where evaluation data, tracing infrastructure, and a measurement pipeline were critical to transitioning a failed chatbot PoC to production, and where observability later caught policy drift causing stale answers.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Rafael Levi presents a case that agents often falsely claim to have searched the web when they hit blocks, CAPTCHAs, or use stale training data. He demonstrates how Bright Data's Web MCP enables real web access on sites like LinkedIn and Instagram, reducing hallucinations and improving citation reliability compared to agents without genuine external access.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Nvidia's Ziv Ilan demonstrated techniques for accelerating video diffusion models to near real-time speeds on Blackwell B200 by reducing denoising steps from 50 to as few as one. The approach combines dynamic quantization with Black Forest Labs, latent chunk caching to avoid recomputation, and step distillation using both trajectory-based and distribution-based training methods. The optimizations are modular and additive, delivering up to 200x speedups when stacked together.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: ChatGPT apps and MCP use nested double iframes to isolate third-party code while preserving origin-indexed storage and preventing CSP bypasses. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which injects the actual app HTML via srcdoc into an inner frame, replicating Facebook's app marketplace pattern.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers at frontier labs use self-prompting loops where an orchestrator agent spawns worker agents in parallel rather than prompting models directly. This approach scales task execution but requires observability tooling and incurs higher token costs as loops run for extended periods.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers. It routes tasks to different models based on job type, includes a governance layer for human oversight, and enables session continuity across devices.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden withdrawal of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model following a reported US government export-control directive allegedly triggered by a claimed jailbreak. Medin provides performance benchmarks he conducted before the model's removal and analyzes implications for developers building on large language models.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates Google's open-source Agents CLI, which enables rapid building and deployment of production-grade AI agents. The tool provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails, integrating with AI coding assistants like Claude Code for agent development.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: After spent 30+ hrs building loops...
videos.summary: AI Jason shares a loop engineer template and free course on building AI agents. The resource includes a GitHub repository with codebase harness and knowledge template, plus a step-by-step workshop in the AI Builder Club.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video covers how to build proactive agents and self-improving systems. Demonstrates autonomous optimization use cases in ads and SEO. Discusses Loopany framework with memory and tools as enablers for these capabilities.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason presents the /goal feature and related tools for autonomous coding workflows. The video covers setup, prompting techniques, and long-horizon task execution through /Mission exploration, positioning these as advances toward true autonomous coding.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a coding framework discussed as a new paradigm for AI-assisted development. The video covers how Symphony works, integration with a codebase harness, automated testing setup, and workflow configuration.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: A 10-minute video tutorial explaining bigram language models as a foundational building block for understanding larger language models like GPT. The content covers tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, and training mechanics including backpropagation and the AdamW optimizer.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series of open models in Nano, Super, and Ultra sizes. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture, MTP, and Latent Mixture of Experts to differentiate from competing open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model incorporating MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) to replace full attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Pi, describing it as a unique agent framework that enables novel use cases like OpenClaw. The video compares Pi against other coding agent tools including Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, examining how it handles complex harness layer requirements.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS provides AI coding agents with tools, documentation, best practices, and guardrails to work effectively with AWS services. It improves success rates by giving agents access to up-to-date information instead of relying on outdated general knowledge.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers walkthrough on running Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool, on Amazon Bedrock. Covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and guardrails for compliance and data residency requirements.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses how to stand out in tech hiring amid AI resume screening. The video covers personal branding, open source contributions, and argues that AI will evolve jobs rather than eliminate them, with junior developers remaining valuable to teams.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS developers guide covering five production best practices for AI agents: streaming tool call events, layered authentication with OAuth and IAM, contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing. Demonstrates patterns applicable across platforms using TypeScript and AWS services.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video explores whether Google engineers use "vibe coding" practices and compares code generation approaches between OpenAI Codex and Claude. The content covers current and projected future vibing trends at Google across 2024 to 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool presented as disruptive to open source dynamics. The video explores how Malus operates and its implications for open source maintainers and the broader ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT Moment" is coming for robotics but the focus on humanoid robots by companies like 1X and Unitree is myopic. She contends that designing for human interaction and delight, not just utility, should guide the deployment of billions of robots entering society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that consolidates a company's finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data into one place to enable better decision-making. Co-founded by Eléonore Crespo, the platform has raised over 400 million dollars and counts major companies like Figma, Brex, and Anthropic as customers. It was created to address the observation that even highly innovative enterprises like Google rely on outdated tools like spreadsheets for strategic planning.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses distinguishing real product-market fit from false signals. He argues that rabid early fans and positive feedback are misleading, real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push, and deep customer embeddedness matters more than formal interviews. He emphasizes betting on AI model trajectory rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization is more viable than subscription models for AI tools and that the real bottleneck for AI agents is human trust adoption rather than technology. He discusses using probability-based decision frameworks learned from competitive sailing to evaluate startup risk.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
